target.name: Vertical Semiconductor
target.founder: Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, Tomas Palacios

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vertical Semiconductor is a Seed-stage chip design company founded by Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, and Tomas Palacios, having raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public semiconductors (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and Rain AI—all operating in the Chips & Semiconductors sector at Seed or Series A stage.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier to the $11M raised proxy, implying a $28M valuation consistent with Seed-to-next-stage advancement in the sector. Peer-Median anchors to the median valuation of four same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables: Etched ($5.0B Series A), d-Matrix ($2.0B Series A), Substrate ($1.0B Series A), and Rain AI ($500M Series A), yielding a $1.5B implied valuation with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the semiconductor sector P/S median of 24.4x cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

The triangulated range spans $23M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $764M. This wide dispersion reflects stage and maturity discounting relative to Series A peers; material uncertainties include technology differentiation, pathway to revenue, and capital efficiency relative to the peer set. The Stage-Progression method suggests a near-term floor; the Peer-Median range bounds longer-term value if the company reaches comparable scaling and market validation.
